page: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the n4d server family. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 CPU architecture and is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU.

This instance type does not include bundled local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through external block storage options. It contains no hardware GPU accelerators and does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the n4d-standard-2 is designed for general-purpose workloads. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance, making it suitable for light web serving, small databases, development environments, and basic microservices that do not require specialized hardware acceleration or local NVMe storage.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-2 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine instance from the n4d server family. It is configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on external network-attached storage for persistent data. With its dedicated CPU resources, the n4d-standard-2 offers predictable performance for entry-level workloads. It is designed for general-purpose applications such as small-scale web serving, microservices, development environments, and lightweight database tasks that require a stable, cost-efficient resource profile without the need for specialized hardware or high-density memory configurations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-2 is a general-purpose x86_64 instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and no bundled local storage, designed for entry-level workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-standard-2 is a general-purpose x86_64 instance with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM for entry-level workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated x86_64 processing cores and a standard memory-to-CPU ratio for predictable entry-level workloads.
bullet_points: 8.0 GB of system memory with 4.0 GB per core
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Predictable performance via dedicated CPU allocation
Suited for light web serving, microservices, and development environments
